description:
Evaluate a 'road diet' (lane reduction) in the Bologna Acosta network.

1. Parse the compiled network file (/home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_acosta/acosta_buslanes.net.xml) to identify the target edge. Exclude internal edges (IDs starting with ':' or function='internal'). Filter for edges with 2 or more lanes. Find the edge with the longest physical length (read from the 'length' attribute of its <lane> elements). If there's a tie, use the first alphabetically by ID.

2. Run the baseline simulation using /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_acosta/run.sumocfg and save trip statistics to /home/ga/SUMO_Output/baseline_tripinfo.xml using the --tripinfo-output flag.

3. Author a plain XML edge patch file at /home/ga/SUMO_Output/roaddiet.edg.xml that updates the target edge to have exactly 1 lane (use the numLanes attribute).

4. Use netconvert to apply your patch file to the original .net.xml file, outputting to /home/ga/SUMO_Output/acosta_roaddiet.net.xml.

5. Create a new SUMO config at /home/ga/SUMO_Output/roaddiet.sumocfg, identical to the original but pointing to the new network. Run it, saving tripinfo to /home/ga/SUMO_Output/roaddiet_tripinfo.xml.

6. Parse both tripinfo XMLs to calculate the average trip duration (sum of 'duration' attributes divided by the number of trips). Create a plain text report at /home/ga/SUMO_Output/road_diet_report.txt containing EXACTLY these lines:
Target_Edge: <edge_id>
Original_Lanes: <integer>
Baseline_Average_Duration: <float>
Diet_Average_Duration: <float>
Duration_Increase: <float>
